title: Employment boost at Glasgow Prestwick following e-commerce growth

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: photo_camera L-R: Ian Forgie, chief executive, Glasgow Prestwick Airport and Alan Gemmell, Member of Parliament (MP) for Central Ayrshire, Scotland Glasgow Prestwick Airport (PIK) has reported a significant rise in job numbers following recent growth in e-commerce industry activity. Ian Forgie, chief executive at PIK, said: “This is a significant increase in headcount at the airport as we expand our services, and is great news for the Airport and the Ayrshire economy. “It has been a busy few months as we have onboarded new staff for these services.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: “The recent addition of scheduled daily e-commerce flights between China and Prestwick means we can play a greater role in Scottish salmon and whisky exports direct from Scotland to China, and I am confident that this will lead to further employment opportunities.” To mark this achievement, Alan Gemmell, Member of Parliament (MP), Central Ayrshire, Scotland, UK, recently visited PIK to see first-hand the new equipment and handling operations ongoing at the airport. He said: “It was brilliant to visit the logistics hub at Prestwick Airport and hear about the new e-commerce contracts with two Chinese flag carriers.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: “I was impressed to learn the team process and offload up to two days faster than any other UK airport and now have 150 e-commerce staff. “The airport continues to go from strength to strength – I look forward to supporting Ian and the team in any way I can.”

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Employment boost at Glasgow Prestwick following e-commerce growth
